question:
Bellman-Ford primarily solves:
A. All-pairs shortest paths B. Single-source shortest paths with possible negative edges C. Minimum spanning tree D. Maximum matching E. I am not sure

Standard Bellman-Ford time complexity is:
A. O(V + E) B. O(E log V) C. O(VE) D. O(V^3 log V) E. I am not sure

Excluding graph storage, Bellman-Ford extra memory is typically:
A. O(1) B. O(V) C. O(E) D. O(VE) E. I am not sure

Bellman-Ford edge relaxation for (u,v,w) is:
A. dist[v] = w unconditionally B. if dist[u] + w < dist[v], then update dist[v] C. Swap dist[u] and dist[v] D. Update only when w is nonnegative E. I am not sure

After k full relaxation passes, Bellman-Ford has best distances among paths using at most:
A. k edges B. k vertices C. k negative edges D. k cycles E. I am not sure

Standard Bellman-Ford negative-cycle check does:
A. After V-1 full relax rounds, run one extra round and test whether any reachable edge can still relax B. Stop immediately when a negative-weight edge is seen C. Check whether any dist[v] is negative D. Check whether total edge weight sum is negative E. I am not sure

Bellman-Ford reliably detects negative cycles that are:
A. Anywhere in the graph regardless of reachability B. Reachable from the chosen source C. Only odd-length cycles D. Only in undirected graphs E. I am not sure

In Bellman-Ford, after V-1 passes, if one more full pass still relaxes an edge, then:
A. Graph is disconnected B. A reachable negative cycle exists C. All distances are finalized correctly D. Only odd cycles exist E. I am not sure

To reconstruct paths after Bellman-Ford, a typical extra array is:
A. color[] B. parent/predecessor[] C. rank[] D. lowlink[] E. I am not sure

Bellman-Ford can terminate early when:
A. The source has no outgoing edges B. All edge weights are nonnegative C. A full pass makes no distance update D. The graph is a DAG E. I am not sure

If all edge weights are nonnegative, Bellman-Ford is:
A. Incorrect B. Correct but often slower than Dijkstra C. Equivalent to Floyd-Warshall D. Faster than BFS on unweighted graphs by definition E. I am not sure

DP view: after k passes, Bellman-Ford has best distances using paths with:
A. Exactly k vertices B. At most k edges C. At most k negative edges D. Exactly k cycles E. I am not sure

Bellman-Ford is best categorized as:
A. Label-setting B. Label-correcting C. Divide-and-conquer D. Backtracking E. I am not sure

Bellman-Ford is algorithmically categorized as:
A. Label-setting B. Greedy tree-growing C. Divide-and-conquer APSP D. Label-correcting E. I am not sure

With V vertices and E edges, standard Bellman-Ford worst-case time complexity is:
A. O(VE) B. O((V+E)logV) C. O(V^2) D. O(E log V) E. I am not sure

Johnson's algorithm uses Bellman-Ford mainly to:
A. Build an MST B. Compute vertex potentials and detect negative cycles C. Perform topological sorting D. Find SCCs E. I am not sure

Each Bellman-Ford full iteration over k (pass) scans:
A. Only outgoing edges of source B. All E edges C. Only edges on current best path tree D. Only negative edges E. I am not sure

Bellman-Ford directly supports:
A. Directed weighted graphs B. Only undirected unweighted graphs C. Only DAGs D. Only complete graphs E. I am not sure

On unweighted graphs (single source), BFS is usually compared with Bellman-Ford as:
A. Asymptotically faster B. Asymptotically slower C. Asymptotically equivalent to O(VE) D. Equivalent only on dense graphs E. I am not sure

Bellman-Ford invariant after exactly k full relaxation passes (k>=0) is:
A. dist stores shortest-path values using at most k edges B. dist stores shortest-path values using exactly k edges C. dist already equals all-pairs shortest paths D. dist is final regardless of k E. I am not sure

Path reconstruction from target t after Bellman-Ford usually follows:
A. Successor links forward from source B. Predecessor links backward from t to source C. Random sampled edges D. Edges in nondecreasing weight order E. I am not sure

Compared with Dijkstra, Bellman-Ford does NOT require:
A. Edge list input B. Nonnegative edge weights C. A source vertex D. Distance initialization E. I am not sure

Bellman-Ford is valid on DAG inputs:
A. No B. Only if all weights are positive C. Yes D. Only for undirected DAGs E. I am not sure

If dist[u] is INF in Bellman-Ford, relaxation of edge (u,v,w) should typically:
A. Set dist[v]=w B. Be skipped C. Set dist[v]=INF+w D. Force dist[v]=0 E. I am not sure

In standard Bellman-Ford shortest-path phase (without early stop), the number of full relaxation rounds is:
A. V-1 B. E-1 C. V D. log V E. I am not sure
How do you solve the shortest path problem when edges may have negative weights?
In practice, is there a shortest path algorithm that can detect negative cycles in the graph?
In practice, what algorithm guarantees correct shortest paths even if some edge weights are negative?
In practice, which algorithm can handle graphs with negative edges without requiring non-negative weights like Dijkstra?
What algorithm would you use to find shortest paths from a single source when edge costs can be negative?
In practice, what approach should be used for SSSP when the graph contains negative-weight edges?
In practice, which algorithm works for arbitrary edge weights and can also identify unreachable negative cycles?
In practice, what is the method that relaxes all edges repeatedly V-1 times to ensure shortest path convergence?
How do you compute shortest paths in a graph that might have negative cycles affecting reachability?
In practice, what algorithm initializes distances to infinity and then iteratively updates them for all edges?
In practice, which shortest path algorithm doesn't require a priority queue and uses simple edge relaxation in loops?
How would you find shortest paths when some roads (edges) have tolls that actually give you money (negative weights)?
In practice, what method checks for negative cycles by performing one additional iteration after V-1 relaxations?
In practice, which algorithm has O(VE) time complexity and can handle graphs with negative edge weights?
In practice, what is the approach that updates distance estimates by considering all edges in each iteration?
